overview: With only one component and no external dependencies, the entire system is implemented in a single phase. The hello-world-web service is fully self-contained — it requires no backing services, databases, or sibling components to function. This makes a bottom-up ordering trivially a single step: build, containerize, and deploy the Go HTTP server directly.

phases.name: Foundation & Delivery
phases.description: The sole component of the system — a stateless Go HTTP server — has no dependencies and constitutes the entire application. It is implemented, containerized, and deployed in this single phase.

components.name: hello-world-web
responsibilities: Serve a fully valid, accessible HTML5 'Hello World' page on GET / with correct Content-Type and HTTP 200 status.
Expose a GET /health liveness endpoint returning JSON {"status":"ok"} for Kubernetes health probes.
Return a well-formed 404 HTML response for all undefined routes.
Suppress the default Go 'Server' response header on all routes to avoid leaking runtime information.
Enforce a stateless, single-binary HTTP server with zero external Go dependencies, listening on port 9090.
components.architectureContext: This is the only component in the system. It is called directly by end users (browsers) on GET / and by Kubernetes liveness/readiness probes on GET /health. There are no upstream services, databases, or external APIs — all logic is self-contained within the Go standard library net/http server. It sits behind a Kubernetes ingress or load balancer that routes public traffic to it.
keyConsiderations: Server header suppression: Go's net/http automatically sets a 'Server: Go' header; wrap every ResponseWriter with a custom secureWriter type that calls Header().Del("Server") before WriteHeader and Write to prevent software version disclosure.

Routing precision: Use a single http.HandleFunc("/", ...) and switch on r.URL.Path explicitly — do NOT register separate catch-all patterns that might conflict, and do NOT use third-party routers (stdlib only).
Panic safety: Wrap the handler dispatch in a deferred recover() to ensure the server never crashes on unexpected input; return HTTP 500 on recovered panics without logging sensitive request data.
Multi-stage Dockerfile: Use golang:1.22-alpine to compile a static binary, then copy only the binary into alpine:3.19 to minimize image size and attack surface; EXPOSE 9090 and use CMD ["./server"].

WCAG AA compliance & responsiveness: The inline CSS must use color #1a1a1a on #ffffff (contrast ratio >16:1), clamp()-based font sizing, overflow-x: hidden on body, and a viewport meta tag to satisfy contrast, readability, and no-horizontal-scroll requirements between 320px–1920px.
components.apiContract:
openapi: 3.0.3
info:
  title: Hello World Web
  description: Serves a Hello World HTML page and health check.
  version: 1.0.0
paths:
  /:
    get:
      summary: Hello World page
      description: Returns the main HTML page with a Hello World greeting.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: HTML page returned successfully.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
  /health:
    get:
      summary: Health check
      description: Returns 200 OK when the service is healthy.
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Service is healthy.
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                type: object
                properties:
                  status:
                    type: string
                    example: ok
  /{path}:
    get:
      summary: Catch-all for undefined routes
      parameters:
        - name: path
          in: path
          required: true
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        '404':
          description: Route not found.
          content:
            text/html:
              schema:
                type: string
